column2: EPIC Fin Fang Foom's Annihilation: Conquest
column27: I was never so happy to have a bagel shuffle. Had 2 master strikes in the first 5 turns.  I did have Fin Fang Foom Demolish 2 rares. I thought I would never see them again, then I got the Cloning Technician and that caused a hero deck shuffle. I ended up getting the rare back that I wanted (Apocalyptic Kitty Pride). Both decks had great KO ability that neither had had more than 3 grey cards by the end.  It was hard to balance the HQ with abomination, schemes and trying to keep tech in for Kitty.
Good round, tough with the extra hero
Very difficult to select heroes with good synergy, master strike made a lot of damage.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.

column27: Lost the first game, almost lost the second one as well. One more Master Strike or Scheme Twist would've ended it, but I was able to combo enough ranged cards to get Galactus down before I lost!

Wow. Just wow. Went into this setup figuring out how not to lose to badly. At turn 20 it looked bleak and I shrugged and started already thinking about the second game. Pulled out a miracle on the final turn with only 4 heroes left in the deck and the fatal scheme twist lurking only a card or 2 away in the villain deck. 2nd setup was about as bad a train wreck as I thought I'd have on the 1st go around. Epic game!

column27: This was awesome. I got 3 rares on the left side and was able to use Investigate and Daredevil's rare to draw my entire hand on the last turn and hit the Mastermind 3 times. 4 of these heroes are pretty new to me too, so it was nice to get them on the table and do so well.

Picked Jean Grey for bystanders and score and Prof X to help her and manipulate the deck.  Easy and game and wish I could've KO'd more on my left hand side to get even more bystanders. Warpath and Daredevil also make a surprisingly great investigate combo.

Villains were surprisingly kept at bay.  We decided to take the L on the first game after the 7th Twist as we were going nowhere.  That decision definitely paid off.

column27: X-Force Wolverine and Warpath in the same deck is an insane combo. They work with each other really well, and they both KO cards. I had all 11 Twists appear despite only getting through 3/4s of the Villain Deck, but it didn't matter.
